title: Japan's long-term interest rate rises to 2.81%, its highest level in 29 years
summary: The increase comes amid expectations of faster inflation and the possibility that the Bank of Japan will delay its rate hike.

title: Kyoto University designated as 'International Research Excellence University' and will receive 200 billion yen
summary: It becomes the third institution to obtain this status, after Tohoku University and Tokyo University of Science, which will allow an academic reorganization.

title: Former director of a materials company arrested for allegedly defrauding a state bank of 1.1 billion yen
summary: The executive submitted false financial reports to hide losses and obtain loans, according to the fiscal investigation.

title: Fifth anniversary of Atami landslide that killed 28 people, with slow progress in reconstruction
summary: The families of the victims continue to demand accountability for the disaster, while 25 people remain displaced.

title: Japan and India sign agreements on artificial intelligence, metals, and energy during bilateral summit
summary: Prime Minister Modi and Prime Minister Takaichi strengthen the strategic partnership, highlighting economic and security cooperation.

title: Yen reaches its weakest level in decades, purchasing power halved in 40 years
summary: The Japanese currency experiences high volatility amid expectations of intervention by the Bank of Japan and divergence from Fed policy.

title: Owner of Causeway Bay bookstore in Hong Kong dies at 70, detained in China for selling critical books
summary: Lam Wing-kee, known for his pro-democracy activism in Hong Kong, died in Taiwan after being released.

title: Japan tightens conditions for permanent residency and suspends reception of 'immediate' foreign workers
summary: The new measures include requiring Japanese language and culture programs, as well as an adjustment in residence fees.

title: US employment grows only 57,000 in June, below expectations, and Dow Jones hits new record
summary: The weak data reinforces the expectation that the Federal Reserve will keep rates unchanged, boosting markets.

title: Portugal defeats Croatia in extra time and advances in the FIFA World Cup
summary: A stoppage-time goal sealed the Portuguese victory in a group stage match of the tournament.

title: Senegal wins a dramatic World Cup match with a goal from I. Sarr
summary: The Senegalese team resisted the pressure and prevailed in extra time, keeping their hopes alive in the tournament.

title: Japan national football team coach Hajime Moriyasu does not confirm his future after the World Cup
summary: The Japan Football Association faces the decision to renew the coach, who stated that 'in the future we can be number one'.

title: Japan's university investment fund posts record profit of 315.8 billion yen
summary: The strong stock market results will allow increased grants to universities such as Tohoku and Tokyo Science.

title: Jungleria amusement park offers free parking to Okinawa residents to attract local visitors
summary: The measure aims to reduce criticism over high parking prices and encourage use among island residents.

title: SoftBank Hawks outfielder Akira Nakamura announces retirement from professional baseball
summary: Nakamura, who was the hits leader in 2014, bids farewell after a career of more than a decade in the NPB.

title: Iran begins funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei and asks US and Israel to avoid attacks during mourning
summary: Thousands attend the procession in Tehran as the country faces international tensions.

title: Fire at Tokyo elementary school caused by teacher's personal heater mobilizes firefighters
summary: The fire started in the teachers' room and caused no injuries, but sparked debate over the safety of personal electrical devices.

title: English translation of Uke-san's mystery book 'Henna E' does not win Dagger Award
summary: The work, which was a finalist, did not win the prestigious British literary award.

title: US celebrates 250th anniversary of independence with fireworks and historical reflections
summary: The commemoration includes events across the country, though it also raises concerns about air quality due to fireworks.

title: Japanese opposition pushes to reduce number of seats in the Diet, while government remains firm
summary: The debate over seat reduction and the proposal of a vice capital creates parliamentary deadlock.
